title: VP of Software Engineering
description:
VP of Software Engineering
Canada, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role is for a high-impact engineering leader who has been in the trenches at fast-scaling startups — someone who has grown from individual contributor to leader and helped scale engineering orgs through multiple phases of growth. Ideally, you've done it more than once.

As VP of Software Engineering, you'll be responsible for building and maintaining a high-performing, scalable engineering organization across Product Development, Ops, and Technical Support. This is a role for someone who’s hands-on enough to spot an issue i

Piping Designers

 

We're seeking skilled and detail-oriented Piping Designers, of all levels, to join our Design & Consulting team in supporting innovative shipbuilding and marine engineering projects!

 

Our team is split into two main divisions that complement each other, 3D modeling and Manufacturing information (MI). The 3D model team takes engineering documents and turns them into a fully integrated digital ship. The MI team takes the 3D model and produces 2D drawings that the shipyard production facilities can fabricate from.

 

In this role, you’ll play a key part in developing piping systems and layouts for new vessel construction and refit programs. Working closely with other disciplines, production teams, and regulatory bodies, you’ll help bring complex designs to life using advanced 3D modeling tools and industry software to produce 2D drawings.

At Genoa, founded in 1995 and a leader in production design and 3D modeling services for the shipbuilding and offshore industries, we believe success is built by people who are passionate, curious, and committed to excellence.

Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

The Calibration Specialist will support the build, calibration, testing and maintenance of Lidar sensor systems. This role is critical to ensuring high quality sensor performance through precise optical alignments, calibration processes, and system level testing. The ideal candidate combines strong technical aptitude with careful execution, thrives in a lab environment and is comfortable working with optics, electronics, and software tools.

Execute full sensor build, calibration and refurbishment workflows following established procedures and flow charts 
Perform optical assembly, alignment, and testing of LiDAR systems and conduct calibration processes, run system integration testing 
Perform incoming inspection on lasers, detectors, scanners and other LiDar components
Diagnose and troubleshoot LiDAR systems in a lab and remotely
Use lab equipment such as oscilloscopes, multimeters and power meters
Troubleshoot communication systems (RS-232

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

Build technology that operates where few others can—deep ocean environments.




At Kraken Robotics, we design advanced subsea imaging, battery, and robotic systems used around the world. We’re growing our engineering team and looking for an Intermediate Electrical Engineer to contribute to the design of mission-critical electronics for next-generation ocean technologies.




You’ll report to the Engineering Manager, Newfoundland.




What You’ll Work On

Design of mixed-signal PCBs and embedded electrical systems for subsea robotics and sensing platforms
Architect electrical systems spanning power, signal integrity, and embedded control
Develop and validate designs for extreme environments (pressure, temperature, EMI)
Collaborate closely with mechanical, firmware, and systems engineers to deliver fully integrated products
Guide products from prototype → production → field deployment
Mentor junior engineers and co-op students

Reporting to the President & Executive Chair, the Managing Director (MD) is the senior operational and leadership executive responsible for the day-to-day performance, stability, and growth of Pilot°. The MD translates vision into execution, ensuring the agency operates profitably, efficiently, and with strategic clarity.

This role is ideal for a seasoned leader who combines business acumen, people leadership, and operational discipline, and who thrives in a creative, client-driven environment. The MD must be a confident, credible leader internally and with senior clients.

The Managing Director is central to Pilot’s next chapter—ensuring the agency is strong, stable, scalable, and future-ready, while preserving the values, reputation, and creative excellence that define the brand for more than three decades.

WHO THEY ARE: 

Pilot° is a full-service marketing and communications agency with more than 30 years of experience supporting long-standing clients across the private, public and non-profit sectors. Small by design, Pilot° brings together a tight-knit team of uniquely talented and experienced professionals who leverage their individual expertise, networks, and trusted relationships to d

title: Senior Full Stack Engineer, Healthcare
description: About UsPropel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don’t.Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We’ve grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.The RoleAre you a Senior Fullstack Engineer looking to build 0→1 products at a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy creating consumer-grade experiences and thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?We’re looking for a motivated Senior Fullstack Engineer to join Propel and help build new Medicare and Medicaid products from the ground up. In this role, you’ll work end-to-end across frontend and backend systems to develop user experiences that help people understand coverage options, surface savings opportunities, and enroll in healthcare plans. You’ll collaborate closely with product and design, and help shape technical architecture, internal tooling, and scalable integrations w

We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.About the RolePropel serves approximately 1M users who are Medicare eligible, most of whom are also Medicaid eligible. We are building the first-ever app-based brokerage focused on helping "duals" (Americans on Medicare and Medicaid) enroll in healthcare plans. We specialize in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and are expanding to 30 states in 2026, with plans to move into larger Medicare markets over the next 24 months. As a Medicare Advisor, you will be a trusted expert, helping users compare Medicare plans, enroll with confidence, and maximize their healthcare benefits. title: Senior Solutions Architect, Government Partnerships
description: About UsPropel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.About the RoleWe're looking for a Solutions Architect to bridge Propel's Government Partnerships team and our Product & Engineering organization - translating state partners' needs into product strategy, and then owning the delivery of that work with states. This role has the opportunity to shape the way our product evolves with deeper government partnerships and increase the value we’re able to deliver to both users and government partners.This is a hands-on IC role. You'll be the person writing the scoping doc, building the dashboard, sitting in the state meeting, configuring the campaign, and running the evaluation. You own both strategy and execution end to end. Over time, as this work scales, the role has potential to grow into a leadership position — but today we need someone who is excited about the execution.You'll work across the state partnership funnel, from early relationship-building through campaign delivery. You should be the kind of person who can walk into a meeting with a state SNAP agency, understand their operational pain points around payment error rates or portal access, walk out with a concrete plan that maps to Propel's product roadmap — and then go execute it. The role reports into our Government Partnerships team,

At C-CORE, we are committed to investing in people and creating a dynamic and rewarding employment experience. C-CORE conducts R&D and provides research-based advisory services, delivering innovative engineering and technology solutions to national and international clients. Over the past 50 years, we have built a world-class team of 70 dedicated professionals with a reputation for excellence in Remote Sensing. We are looking for outstanding engineers and scientists to join our team.

Position Overview

This position requires an engineering degree with experience in CubeSat development, particularly in systems engineering tasks related to payload development, integration, testing and verification.

title: Saint John Litigation Lawyer
description: Are you an experienced litigator with a passion for law and a desire to contribute to diverse, impactful legal matters? Do you want to be part of a dynamic and growing firm that values both personal and professional development?McInnes Cooper is currently seeking an experienced Litigation Lawyer to join our thriving team in Saint John, New Brunswick, where you will have the opportunity to engage in a broad range of complex litigation matters. Our litigation team provides highly diversified services to clients across various industries, regularly appearing in courts at all levels in the Atlantic Provinces, as well as in the Federal Court and the Supreme Court of Canada. Additionally, the team frequently represents clients in administrative and regulatory tribunals, arbitrations, and mediations.As a Litigation Associate, your key responsibilities will include:Managing your own files while collaborating with colleagues on complex legal matters.Providing legal services across a wide array of areas, such as general/civil litigation, commercial litigation, estate litigation, insurance defense, and construction/engineering litigation.Conducting legal research, providing legal opinions, and maintaining up-to-date knowledge on relevant legal developments.Communicating effectively with clients, colleagues, and opposing counsel to ensure successful outcomes.Representing clients in court, at hearings, and in negotiations.The successful candidate will possess the following qualifications:

title: Halifax Labour & Employment Legal Assistant
description: If you're an experienced administrative professional who enjoys working alongside a collaborative team, takes pride in being a trusted partner, and excels at delivering exceptional client service in a fast-paced environment, this role offers the opportunity to learn, grow, and play a key role supporting both our clients and our team.McInnes Cooper is seeking a highly organized, experienced Legal Assistant to join our Labour & Employment team in our Halifax office. As a part of the team, you will provide high-quality administrative support and contribute to an exceptional client service experience.As a Legal Assistant, you will be responsible for:·Working in a team environment assisting in the daily functions of a dynamic Labour & Employment practice group.Providing executive level administrative support, including filing, photocopying, drafting correspondence, transcribing dictation and preparing documentation.Providing proactive calendar management, including the planning, organization, and coordination of meetings, conference calls, and events.Managing and responding to a high volume of correspondence via email and phone.Reviewing, proof reading, and preparing client and other business materials, including client documents, reports, memos, briefings, and presentations.Coordinating and arranging travel.Preparing expenses and billing for processing, ensuring timely and accurate entry of billable time.Challenging yourself and your team every day; and having fun while doing so!

title: Academic-Industry Partnerships, Advisor - Queen’s University
description:
Do you want to be part of a mission driven organization shaping Canada’s innovation future? Do you thrive in collaborative environments where creativity, continuous improvement, and strategic thinking are valued? Are you motivated by work that strengthens Canada’s competitiveness and delivers meaningful impact for communities and businesses? 
 
At Mitacs, we take a talent first approach to building Canada’s innovation capacity. We connect researchers with organizations across every region of the country to help solve pressing challenges and accelerate economic growth. By enabling business academic collaboration and supporting the development of highly skilled talent, we contribute to a resilient, future ready Canadian economy. 
 
Guided by our values — Impact, Integrity, Collaboration, Excellence, and Talent — we work with partners nationwide to advance research, commercialization, and innovation. 
 
At Mitacs, you become part of a team committed to empowering Canadian talent, fostering meaningful partnerships, and driving innovation that supports Canada’s long-term competitiveness. If you’re inspired by big ideas and passionate about creating national impact, we encourage you to apply.

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description: About UsPropel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.The RoleAre you a Platform Engineer looking to make a big impact on a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy architecting software that shapes an entire engineering organization? Do you thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?We’re looking for a self-motivated Senior Platform Engineer to join our Developer Enablement Team within the engineering org at Propel.This role empowers other engineers by improving the cloud native platforms that enable development and delivery of Propel’s technology. These cloud native platforms impact several products and stacks, including interacting with government programs, surfacing savings opportunities, enrolling users in healthcare plans, internal tooling, architecture, and more! Your focus will be on running fault tolerant applications in Kubernetes and AWS, and solving organization-wide engineering problems. We are looking for a tinkerer to experiment with improvements to the developer experience at Propel. You will architect new features for our cloud platform, support a wide-variety of production applications, address technical debt, and help guide other engineers.What we offer:An engineering culture that is humble, motivated, and collaborative Consistent knowledge sharing through projects or bugs demos, Lunch & Lea

title: AI Software Engineer
description: Be a part of our success story. Launch offers talented and motivated people the opportunity to do the best work of their lives in a dynamic and growing company. Through competitive salaries, outstanding benefits, internal advancement opportunities, and recognized community involvement, you will have the chance to create a career you can be proud of. Your new trajectory starts here at Launch! Launch is actively seeking qualified, energetic engineers with passion for building solutions leveraging new and emerging technologies related to AI. This is a software engineering role specializing in applications with use cases powered by AI solutions, especially Generative AI, such as LLM integration, vector embeddings, real-time inference, and semi-automated, human-in-the-loop workflows. This role offers an exciting opportunity to be at the forefront of AI technology, working on diverse projects that drive real-world impact. If you’re passionate about AI and have the technical expertise to back it up, this role may be perfect for you! Responsibilities Include: Write high-quality, maintainable code in languages such as Python, JavaScript, C#, or others relevant to AI development Work closely with and in cross-functional teams including software engineers, project managers, designers, QA, data engineers, and data scientists Integrate with a variety of different APIs, services, and technologies to bring pre-trained models and other technologies to bear, such as cloud-based vector databas

SENIOR PROGRAMMER

Do you dream of having a say in the software you write? Strong Data Automation is an old company with a start up mentality that currently specializes in waste management logistics. That’s garbage you say? Exactly! We are a tight team working to build essential, usable products for our customers quickly and everyone pitches in to get the job done right. You’ll work on problems of secure data collection, operation scaling and automation, and hardware integrations across multiple applications and geographical locations. Customers will love you for making their job about high level decision making instead of copy and pasting between spreadsheets. We handle tracking, billing, trending and more as we try to revolutionize how organizations control what happens to everything the world doesn’t want.

5+ years programming experience
 
Computer science degree or equivalent experience / education
 
Full stack programming experience working primarily in the .NET ecosystem
 
Familiar with modern development best practices and design patterns
 
Knowledge of key security fundamentals for web application development
 
Strong knowledge of SQL Server database
 
Thrives in an atmosphere of taking on new challenges and developing new skills

Careers at Virtual Marine
WHO ARE WE?

Virtual Marine is a privately held Canadian company with its headquarters in Paradise, Newfoundland and Labrador.  Our mission is to improve the safety of life at sea by providing realistic, effective, and safe training tools for small craft operators. Our simulators allow crews of small marine craft to train for real-life situations in environments that are often too dangerous and costly for live boat exercises. 

We’re growing our strong, cohesive, aligned team with a can-do attitude, that’s driven by our values. We foster an inclusive culture and it shows - staff turnover is lower than industry average. We celebrate wins, and take care of each other. 

WHAT DO WE OFFER?

We offer a competitive salary and benefits package and a rewarding challenge with our dynamic and growing team solving big problems!

Interested in seeing some of our work? 
Visit our YouTube channel @VMTechnology or check out the rest of our website or social media.

description:

Permanent Full Time Location: Remote (open to applicants in the following states: AL, AR, IL, LO, MO, MS, TX)
Grow Your Earnings. Close Lucrative Deals. Build Your Career.

At Vision33, we are looking for a driven Account Executive who is motivated by building relationships, closing net-new business, and significantly growing their income through performance. 

High performers thrive here. With our uncapped commission potential, strong sales support, and a proven delivery organization, Account Executives can substantially and consistently grow their income year over year.


The Role:
As an Account Executive, you will take ownership of the full sales cycle for net-new ERP software opportunities, developing and executing strategies that generate pipeline, win new clients, and exceed revenue targets.
You will:

Drive net-new revenue growth by developing and managing a strong pipeline of qualified opportunities.
Prospect and engage new clients, building relationships with decision-makers from operations leaders to C-suite executives.
Forecast demand and manage your pipeline to consistently exceed monthly and annual sales targets.
Position Vision33’s full suite of solutions and services, ensuring prospects clearly understand the value of partnering with us.
Lead compelling sales presentations and solution discussions, translating complex technology into clear business outcomes.
Collaborate with regional leadership, sales and delivery team to craft winnin

This is an exciting opportunity to join at the ground level and help define what success looks like. As Vision33’s newest Solutions Engineer, you’ll have the ability to make an immediate impact, influence how we grow, and build your career alongside a rapidly expanding cloud practice. This role also includes performance-based incentives tied to key business objectives.

As part of this strategic team, you’ll partner closely with both sales and delivery teams — helping shape solution strategy during pre-sales engagements, leading compelling demonstrations, and supporting successful customer outcomes. You’ll work alongside experienced leaders in a collaborative, fast-moving environment where storytelling, innovation, and a cloud-first mindset are highly valued.

Nous travaillons en partenariat avec des organisations en croissance dans les secteurs public et privé pour comprendre leur activité et les aider à réaliser leur vision avec la bonne combinaison de stratégie, de conseil et de technologie. Les membres de l’équipe hautement qualifiés et axés sur les résultats de Vision33 fournissent une expertise transparente en matière de processus commerciaux et de technologie dans divers secteurs et groupes de clients. 
 
Vision33 Canada Inc., un partenaire primé de Sage Intacct, a été nommé gagnant du Sage Platinum Club pour l'exercice 2023.  Vision33 est actuellement à la recherche d'un consultant en implantation de Sage Intacct (bilingue) pour rejoindre notre équipe. Si vous recherchez une opportunité passionnante, nous avons un rôle pour vous!  Dans ce rôle pratique, vous serez responsable de : 

Fournir des services de conseil, de la formation et de l'assistance pour assurer aux clients des déploiements réussis de solutions logicielles de comptabilité en infonuagique. 
Travailler directement avec les directeurs financiers, les directeurs informatiques et les utilisateurs finaux pour façonner des solutions qui accèdent aux informations

title: Cloud IT Consultant
description:
Cloud IT Consultant, Cloud AWS ServicesFull Time / Permanent

Ready to take cloud deployments to the next level? Join our fast-growing team as an AWS Cloud IT Consultant, where you’ll design, deploy, and optimize AWS environments for SAP Business One and SAGE clients.

You’ll work hands-on with Linux and Windows servers, manage web and application services, and support client go-lives—even after hours. Your expertise will help shape scalable, secure cloud systems that drive real business results.

What You’ll Do:

Deploy AWS environments for SAP & SAGE clients.
Configure and support web and application services (IIS, Apache, Nginx) and file/print services.
Lead go-live migrations, including after-hour cutovers. 


Document build processes, adhere to policies, develop and maintain procedural documentation, and support continuous improvement. 


Collaborate with clients, consultants, and stakeholders to ensure successful builds and deployments.


Monitor system and database backups to ensure processes are running as expected.
Complete environmental restorations and server maintenance.
Respond to security events and resolve issues. 
Identify potential issues and drive proactive solutions.


Experience Required for the Role:

5+ years’ AWS or other cloud deployment.
Strong Linux skills (SUSE preferred) and solid Windows/AD/Networking knowledge (VPN, Firewalls, AWS Security groups).
Deployment of Windows Servers (MS SQL, RDP, DC, GTW, WEB, etc.).
Familiarity with SAP HANA and ERP

Sales development – Introductory calls to customer engagements
Systems deployment – administrative and support
Marketing coordination – materials and presentations
Excellent written/verbal communication skills
Technical competence with basic MS Office applications

title: Mechanical Engineer
description:
Mechanical Engineer
Canada, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.
CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.
Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post.

About the Role

We’re hiring a Mechanical Engineer to help shape the future of engineering collaboration.

This is not a traditional mechanical engineering role focused on designing physical products. Instead, you’ll work at the intersection of engineering, software, and customer problem-solving — helping ensure CoLab deeply understands how modern engineering teams actually work.

You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Design, Sales, and Customer teams to bring real engineering context into everything we build. Your job is to help bridge the g

Litigation Paralegal
Litigation Paralegal | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking a permanent, full-time Litigation Paralegal to join our Halifax office to provide paralegal services to the Litigation Practice Groups.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Draft pleadings,
Utilize Litigation Support Software to manage document production, disclosure and organization, including the following tasks:
data entry to objectively code documents
preparation of Affidavits Disclosing Documents (including review and redact for privilege and relevance);
compile Books of Evidence/Exhibits;
document production;
import documents received from opposing counsel;
prepare legal chronologies;
Record all billable and non-billable functions on a daily basis;
Utilize computer technology in an efficient and productive manner;
General office and clerical duties, including billable and non-billable functions;
Other duties as required.
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

Minimum 5 years’ experience as a litigation paralegal/

for Noseworthy Chapman – Chartered Professional Accountants, located in St. John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador.

WHO WE ARE: 

Noseworthy Chapman is a team of professionals who share an entrepreneurial spirit for success. We’ve chosen to build our careers and raise our families in Newfoundland and Labrador, giving us a grounded, local perspective on what it takes to live, work, and thrive here.

We are the largest independent firm of Chartered Professional Accountants in the province, with a team of more than 50. We pride ourselves on delivering practical, forward-thinking advice to help our clients operate, grow, and succeed in an ever-changing economic landscape.

title: Associate Vice-President, Human Resources
description:
The University of Lethbridge invites applications for the position of Associate Vice-President (AVP), Human Resources. This is a significant leadership opportunity to advance people, culture, and organizational effectiveness at a pivotal time for the University. 

The Organization

Founded in 1967, the University of Lethbridge is a comprehensive academic and research institution recognized for its commitment to liberal education, experiential learning, and community engagement. With campuses in Lethbridge and Calgary, the University serves approximately 8,000 students and employs a complex workforce of faculty and staff represented across eight different employee groups. Guided by a new strategic plan and energized by recent academic program expansions, including the launch of a new medical program and the planned development of an engineering program, the University is poised for transformative growth.

The University of Lethbridge’s Blackfoot name is Iniskim, meaning Sacred Buffalo Stone. The University is located in traditional Blackfoot Confederacy territory. We honour the Blackfoot people and their traditional ways of knowing in caring for this land, as well as all Indigenous Peoples who have helped shape and continue to strengthen our University community.

The Role
The Associate Vice-President (AVP), Human Resources, is the University’s senior human resources leader, responsible for advancing a strategic, people-centred, service-oriented HR function that supports the a

title: Interim Executive Director
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, CA
description:
Interim Executive Director
Single Parent’s Association of Newfoundland and Labrador (SPANL)
St. John’s, NL

Step into a role where strong leadership meets real-world impact, guiding an organization that changes lives every day.

The Company

SPANL was founded in September of 1987 by Margaret Acreman. Based on her own experience as a single parent, she recognized the gaps in available services and little awareness of the issues facing single parent families.  Today, the Single Parent Association of Newfoundland and Labrador (SPANL) is a dynamic, non-profit organization dedicated to empowering single parents on their journey to overcome challenges and achieve their goals. Through a comprehensive range of programs and services—including education, emergency assistance, and community supports—SPANL fosters self-sufficiency and resilience in families across the province.

SPANL creates a strong sense of belonging through social initiatives, peer support, and volunteer engagement. With a mission to elevate the quality of life for single parent families while promoting sustainability, collaboration, and inclusivity, SPANL continues to play a vital and respected role in Newfoundland and Labrador’s social fabric.

The Role 

This is a full-time, one-year term position offered as maternity leave coverage.

Reporting to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director is responsible for the overall leadership, operational management, and financial stewardship of SPANL. This includes ensur

title: Vice-President, Finance and Operations
description:
The University of Windsor is seeking an experienced and values-driven leader to serve as its Vice-President, Finance and Operations (VPFO). This current vacancy is a pivotal opportunity to shape the future of the University through strategic, innovative, and collaborative leadership in support of UWindsor’s academic mission and long-term success.

The Organization

The University of Windsor is situated on the traditional territory of the Three Fires Confederacy of First Nations: the Ojibwa, the Odawa, and the Potawatomi.

The University is a comprehensive research and teaching institution with more than 16,000 students. Located in the heart of Canada's Border City, the University of Windsor combines excellence in teaching, research, and professional practice with a commitment to build stronger communities, treat people with respect and dignity, and address pressing societal needs. 

The Role

Reporting to the President and Vice-Chancellor, the Vice-President, Finance and Operations (VPFO) provides strategic, ethical, and people-centred leadership across the University’s administrative and operational portfolios. These include finance, facilities, information technology, ancillary services, environmental sustainability, enterprise risk management, and campus safety.

As a key member of the senior leadership team, the VPFO works in close partnership with the Provost to advance integrated planning and decision-making that aligns resources with academic priorities and long-term i

title: Vice President, Strategic Academic Initiatives
description:
Yorkville University is seeking a strategic, innovative, and collaborative academic leader to serve as its next Vice-President, Strategic Academic Initiatives. This is a unique opportunity to shape and advance transformative academic priorities, institutional partnerships, and future-focused initiatives that strengthen student success and organizational impact.

Yorkville University
At Yorkville University and Toronto Film School, we believe education is more than the pursuit of knowledge – it is a catalyst for transformation. Our mission, grounded in democratizing education, is to create access for learners from every background and empower them to achieve their aspirations and advance their careers.

We are committed to delivering rigorous, flexible programs that are both personally fulfilling and professionally rewarding, while contributing to the betterment of society. Since 2004, Yorkville has been among Canada’s few private, for-profit, fully accredited degree-granting universities, with campuses in Ontario, British Columbia, and New Brunswick. Together with Toronto Film School, we offer creative and academic pathways at both the college and university levels, cultivating a multidisciplinary environment where innovation and the arts converge.

Our community is vibrant and diverse. Many of our learners are adults balancing education with careers and personal commitments. Every member of our team – whether in teaching, operations, or technology – is dedicated to removing

title: SAP Business One Production Consultant, Brutos ERP
description:
SAP Business One Production Consultant, Brutos ERP 
Full Time / Permanent Location:Open Canada – East Coast  Language Requirement: English (Preference for English with minimum conversational French) 
 
Ready to combine your SAP Business One expertise with your passion for craft beverages? 

Brutos by Vision33 is growing, and we’re looking for an experienced SAP Business One (Production-focused) Implementation Consultant to help breweries and distilleries streamline operations with our industry-focused ERP solution powered by SAP Business One. 

Brutos by Vision33 is a beverage ERP software that helps brewery and distillery manufacturers increase operational efficiency and gain 360-degree visibility into their business. It is an all-in-one ERP software that harnesses the power of SAP Business One to centralize data by combining front- and back-office functions.  

If you thrive in customer-facing ERP consulting roles and love solving complex production and manufacturing challenges, this could be your next great opportunity. 

Your blend of responsibilities: 

Lead full-cycle SAP Business One implementations, upgrades, and migrations for brewery and distillery customers.  


Guide clients through business process improvements and identify system gaps and optimization opportunities.  


Build strong customer relationships through ongoing consultation and problem-solving.  


Configure solutions, create documentation and reports, and support successful rollouts.  


Collaborate w

title: Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) Analyst
description:
Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) Analyst

Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) Analyst

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently recruiting for an Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) Analyst to join our Dartmouth, Nova Scotia office or our Rostock, Germany office.




ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Support ILS related activities, such as: Reliability Analysis, Failure Modes Effects & Criticality Analysis (FMECA), Reliability Centered Maintenance Analysis (RCMA), Maintenance Task Analysis (MTA), Level of Repair Analysis (LORA) and Life Cycle Costing (LCC) Analysis
Perform Provisioning related activities, such as: Sparing Analysis, Parts Provisioning Breakdowns (PPB), Recommended Spare Parts List (RSPL), Long Lead Time Item Lists (LLTIL)
Development and maintenance of the Logistics Support Analysis Record (LSAR)
Proofread, edit, and format documents of the package, standardizing both content and layout with boilerplate templates in accordance with the client’s applicable style manuals, guidelines, and standards
Maintaining a customer focus to ensure that requirements and expectations are delivered on time and to a high standard

title: Senior Corporate Secretary
description:
Headquartered in Bermuda, with long-standing offices in Halifax and Toronto, Allshores Limited is a publicly traded insurance, health, and wealth management organization and holding company listed on the Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX). Formed through the 2025 amalgamation of BF&M and Argus, Allshores brings together nearly 200 years of combined experience to create a stronger, unified organization serving communities across Bermuda, the Caribbean, Europe, and North America.

As they continue to evolve and grow, Allshores is seeking a Senior Corporate Secretary to play a key role in supporting the effective operation of the Boards of Directors across their group of companies. Serving as a liaison between the Boards, executive leadership team, subsidiary entities, and external stakeholders, you will be responsible for coordinating board and committee activities, maintaining corporate records, supporting regulatory requirements, and facilitating the timely flow of information across multiple jurisdictions. You will also provide leadership and oversight to the Corporate Officer role, ensuring the effective delivery of corporate secretarial and board administration activities across the organization.

This is an exceptional opportunity for an experienced corporate secretary to work closely with senior leadership and Boards of Directors within a complex, multi-jurisdictional organization with an international footprint.

General Manager

Higher Talent is excited to conduct a search for a dynamic, forward thinking General Manager for The City of Corner Brook’s Civic Centre.

WHO WE ARE:

Corner Brook, the garden city and hub of enriched history is located on the western shores of Newfoundland, at the mouth of the Bay of Islands. With a spectacular combination of ocean and mountain scenery, four seasons, and friendly people, Corner Brook is known around the world as both a popular tourist attraction and one of the best places to live, work, and do business. There is plenty of local shopping and restaurants in the city. Corner Brook is also known for its outdoor facilities, such as Marble Mountain Ski Resort. All this in the friendliest climate in Canada, surrounded by some of the most beautiful wilderness in the world.

To learn more about us, please visit The City of Corner Brook.

THE ROLE:

The General Manager of the Civic Centre is a dynamic, forward-thinking and strategic leader responsible for the overall management and operation of the City’s premier multi-purpose facility. title: Vice-President, Strategic Growth, Productivity & Innovation
description:
Humber Polytechnic is seeking a visionary leader to serve as their Vice-President, Strategic Growth, Productivity & Innovation (VP, SGPI).

This is a rare opportunity to shape the future of a leading Canadian polytechnic while influencing the evolving role of post-secondary education in driving economic growth, productivity, and social impact. Operating at the centre of Humber Polytechnic’s strategic agenda, the VP, SGPI will be uniquely positioned to design and lead an integrated growth model that brings together education, industry, government, and global partners in new and transformative ways.

At a time of profound disruption across higher education, driven by changing learner expectations, technological advancement, and shifting economic conditions, this role offers the chance to build something fundamentally new. The VP will architect Humber’s next-generation growth engine, redefining how institutions expand access, diversify revenue, and deliver lifelong learning at scale. Central to this mandate is the ability to drive measurable growth in revenue through the development of new market opportunities, innovative partnership models, and scalable education and service offerings, including those delivered through complex domestic and international collaborations and contractual arrangements. Through this work, the VP will extend Humber’s reach and impact while positioning the institution as a national leader in productivity and a global innovator in polytechnic education.

title: Chief Administrative Officer
description:
Located along the scenic Saint John River in southern New Brunswick, the Town of Grand Bay-Westfield is a growing and vibrant community recognized for its strong quality of life, natural beauty, and close-knit, family-oriented character. Situated just outside the City of Saint John, the Town offers its 6,000 residents and businesses the benefits of a welcoming small-town environment along with strong regional connectivity and access to urban amenities. Formed through the amalgamation of the former communities of Grand Bay and Westfield, the Town has evolved into a progressive municipality focused on sustainable growth, responsible governance, and community vitality.

The Town of Grand Bay – Westfield is seeking a Chief Administrative Officer (CAO). This position serves as the senior administrative leader of the Town and is accountable for the effective and efficient management of all municipal operations. The CAO provides objective and strategic advice to Council and ensures the implementation of policies, priorities, and programs in alignment with legislative and governance frameworks as well as the strategic plan.

Overseeing all municipal departments, the CAO leads a collaborative and well-established leadership team. The organization is characterized by a strong organizational structure, sound governance, a corporate culture that is well appreciated and has an appetite for growth. title: Vice-President, University Relations
description:
First Nations University of Canada (FNUniv) invites applications for the role of Vice President, University Relations. This role is central to amplifying FNUniv’s story, strengthening institutional reputation, advancing fundraising and revenue diversification, and building respectful, reciprocal relationships with Indigenous and non-Indigenous communities and partners.

About First Nations University of Canada

First Nations University of Canada (FNUniv) is a national leader in Indigenous-led post-secondary education. FNUniv is committed to transformative education rooted in Indigenous Knowledge. With four campuses across Saskatchewan, the university provides a learning environment that integrates Indigenous perspectives, cultures, and histories while preparing students for success. As a federated college of the University of Regina, FNUniv offers undergraduate and graduate programs in business, humanities, social sciences, and sciences—all incorporating Indigenous worldviews, traditions, and languages.

Founded in 1976 as the Saskatchewan Indian Federated College (SIFC), FNUniv was renamed in 2003, reinforcing its commitment to Indigenous-led post-secondary education. FNUniv was created so Indigenous learners could study in places where they feel a genuine sense of belonging — where our Indigenous knowledges, languages, and worldviews guide teaching, learning, and research. About BreatheSuite
BreatheSuite delivers virtual pulmonary rehabilitation to patients with chronic lung conditions across the country. Our mission is to make quality pulmonary rehabilitation accessible, empowering patients to take control of their respiratory health from the comfort of their own homes. We use a multidisciplinary model — NPs, physical therapists, and respiratory therapists — supported by a remote therapeutic monitoring platform built specifically for this population.

The Role
We are looking for an experienced Nurse Practitioner to join our virtual care team. BreatheSuite NPs conduct telehealth evaluations and provide ongoing management for patients across the spectrum of chronic lung conditions — including COPD, ILD, and post-COVID — while overseeing respiratory therapists and collaborating with physical therapists as part of a multidisciplinary care team. This is an opportunity to practice cutting-edge digital health delivery, providing 1-on-1 personalized care entirely via telehealth.

Sequence Bio is a Newfoundland and Labrador-based biotechnology company conducting genetic research to discover the true biological causes of disease. 
We collaborate with the people and medical professionals of Newfoundland and Labrador, and partner with leading pharma and biotech to help accelerate the development of better medicines for global unmet medical needs.

title: Customer Education Lead
description:
Customer Education Lead
Canada and US, Remote
Apply

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner. Our product, CoLab, is the world’s first Design Engagement System (DES) - a category defining product that Engineering teams use to engage in meaningful, productive design conversations, catch preventable mistakes, and get to market faster. Our customers include the largest engineering organizations in the world such as Ford, Johnson Controls, Komatsu, and Polaris in the industrial equipment, consumer products, automotive, aerospace & defense, and shipbuilding industries.

We’re building a world-class customer education program to help every customer adopt faster, achieve measurable outcomes, and renew with confidence. This is a foundational role of that function. 

The Customer Education Lead will design and deliver scalable learning experiences that extend the reach of our Customer Success team, accelerate time-to-value, and create consistent, high-quality enablement across our customer base. You'll partner closely with Product (who owns in-product experience), Customer Success (who understand customer pain points and workflows), our Implementation Specialist (who delivers hands-on onboarding and sees firsthand where customers struggle), and Marketing (who own brand voice and external content) to build an education infrastructure that supports the entirety of our customer base from first login to advanced use cases.

description:
Senior Product Designer
Canada, Remote

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named  Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This isn’t just a design role—it’s a leadership role within our UX design org. As a UX Designer, you’ll be an integral design contributor across our various Build Teams You’ll be responsible for many of the most technically demanding and strategically critical areas of the product.

You’ll go deep on complex workflows, integrations, and system interfaces. Your work will shape how our largest customers interact with CoLab—and set the foundation for how we scale our product across global organizations.

description: At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. We actively foster an environment where the unique backgrounds of our associates are valued and celebrated. Our greatest strength lies in the rich blend of culture, talent, and experiences of our associates. We are committed to non-discrimination on any protected basis, including disability, veteran status, or other basis protected by applicable law.At Le Méridien, we are inspired by the era of glamorous travel, celebrating each culture through the distinctly European spirit of savouring the good life. Our guests are curious and creative, cosmopolitan culture seekers that appreciate moments of connection and slowing down to savour the destination. We provide authentic, chic and memorable service along with experiences that inspire guests to savour the good life. We’re looking for curious and creative people to join our team. If you appreciate connecting with like-minded guests and have a deep desire to create memorable experiences, we invite you to explore career opportunities with Le Méridien. In joining Le Méridien, you join a portfolio of brands with Marriott International. Be where you can do your best work,​ begin your purpose, belong to an amazing global​ team, and become the best version of you.

JOB PORTAL

 Job Posts Head Of Engineering / Founding CTO – AI SaaS

HEAD OF ENGINEERING / FOUNDING CTO – AI SAAS

We’re building a local team and want to work as a team together, and to collaborate in person as needed, so this role is for candidates in (or willing to relocate to / regularly commute to) the Halifax area.

About the opportunity:

We’re an early-stage, founder-owned Canadian SaaS startup building an AI-powered social-media marketing and lead-generation platform. The product automates on-brand, compliant content and turns it into qualified leads, a space the current tools don’t serve well.

The founder is looking for the most senior technical person: the owner of architecture and engineering, working shoulder-to-shoulder with the founder to turn the vision into a real, shippable product, and to build the team that ships it. 

This starts as a fractional engagement (about 1 – 2 days/week) and is designed to convert to a full-time Head of Engineering / CTO role as we ramp. We’re looking for a builder-leader, not an advisor.

Key responsibilities:

Technical strategy & architecture: Own the end-to-end architecture (frontend, backend, data, and AI layer) and the major build-vs-buy decisions, e.g., starting on a unified social-posting API and bringing high-volume integrations in-house as we scale.
AI / ML approach: Design a vendor-flexible AI layer, model routing, retrieval (RAG), a lead-qualifyi

 Job Posts Senior Product Designer (UX/UI) AI SaaS – Contract

SENIOR PRODUCT DESIGNER (UX/UI) AI SAAS – CONTRACT

We’re building a local team and want to work as a team together, and to collaborate in person as needed, so this role is for candidates in (or willing to relocate to / regularly commute to) the Halifax area.

About the opportunity:

We’re an early-stage, founder-owned Canadian SaaS startup building an AI-powered social-media marketing and lead-generation platform. The product automates on-brand, compliant content and turns it into qualified leads, a space the current tools don’t serve well.

We are looking for a Senior Technical Product Designer to shape how the whole thing looks and works, and to give our engineers a clear blueprint to build against.

You’ll work directly with the founder and CTO. This is a contract engagement, front-loaded: intensive for roughly the first 2 – 4 months (≈2 – 4 days/week) building the core, then tapering to part-time to support the build.

What you’ll deliver:

The core user flows and information architecture, how our subscribers/professionals set up their brand, generates content, schedules it, and captures leads.
Wireframes and key screens: profile/persona setup, the content-generation and editor screen, the calendar/scheduler, and the lead-capture pages.
A reusable design system and template system subscribers can personalize while staying on b

title: Data Operations Specialist
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description:
Enaimco’s Asset Lifecycle Management (ALM) platform is the place where integrity work happens, embedding daily operations inside a living system of understanding. By connecting physical assets to relevant, searchable digital data, we embed operational workflows directly inside a continuously updating digital twin. This transforms manual data reconciliation into automated clarity, ensuring every engineering decision is based on a current, complete view.

That complete view is just the foundation. By turning asset data into a continuously learning system, our platform delivers contextualized insights today and lays the groundwork for predictive decision-making tomorrow.
We’re looking for a Data Operations Specialist to own how customer data is structured, cleansed, mapped, and validated as it moves into our platform. This is the role that sets the standard for how we handle the messy industrial data our customers bring us, and the discipline that lets every subsequent implementation be faster, more traceable, and more reliable than the last. You’ll be the person who decides how data should be organized for ingestion and ongoing use, then holds the line on those rules across every project.

Operations Support Specialist
Dairy Farmers of Newfoundland and Labrador
St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada

About Us

The Dairy Farmers of Newfoundland and Labrador (DFNL) is a non-governmental organization committed to the sustainable production and marketing of milk across the province. Since 1983, we’ve proudly served as the voice for local dairy farmers, advocating for their interests at both the national and provincial levels. We ensure fair production practices, policy alignment, and effective supply chain coordination to benefit our farmers, partners, and communities.




Our close collaboration with organizations like the School Milk Foundation of Newfoundland and Labrador demonstrates our commitment to health, education, and the well-being of future generations.




Overview of the Role

The Operations Specialist plays a key operational role in the organization, supporting the daily coordination of the provincial transportation division. Reporting directly to the General Manager, you will coordinate the safe and efficient movement of milk throughout the province by monitoring daily schedules, resolving collection and delivery disruptions, and maintaining tracking data to support operational excellence.

ethree Consulting is proud to partner with Campia Gymnastics in their search for a full-time Executive Director.

Through both competitive and recreational programs, we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive, supportive environment where participants of all ages and abilities feel welcome.




What You’ll Do

You will support our members and stakeholder needs while aligning with Campia’s mission, vision, and strategic objectives.

Manager, Business Liaison Office

Who We Are

Located in Labrador City, Labrador West Chamber of Commerce (LWCC) has been Labrador West’s business and regional economic development voice for more than 50 years. With its strong advocacy efforts and highly successful initiatives, the LWCC has been the voice of our business community. As a non-profit, non-partisan association, we take pride in having the opportunity to continuously play a lead role in representing our respective membership, boosting economic prosperity and quality of life in Labrador City and Wabush.

 

The new Business Liaison Office reflects LWCC’s commitment to supporting its members by serving as a central hub for connection, collaboration, and advocacy - promoting LWCC’s role as the link between businesses and all levels of government to drive growth and development across Labrador. For more information, please visit www.labradorwestchamber.com.

 

The LWCC is seeking a strategic and collaborative leader to serve as Manager of the Business Liaison Office for the region. Our Approach

Stewart McKelvey has a long history of being a leader in Atlantic Canada. And being a leader means embracing innovation.

We changed the traditional approach to law student recruitment in Atlantic Canada. Typically, Articled Clerks wait for “hire-back” decisions well into their articling year.

Stewart McKelvey is thinking differently.

We believe that success in the practice of law requires making a commitment for more than one summer or one year. Our innovative approach removes the traditional “hire-back” decision-making process, typically done at the end of the articling year. A 2L Summer Student will advance to becoming an Articled Clerk, then directly to an Associate at Stewart McKelvey.

Controller

 

Your next career move starts here!

 

Genoa Design International (Genoa) is hiring a Controller to join our dynamic Finance & Accounting Team in Mount Pearl, Newfoundland.

 

ABOUT US

At Genoa, founded in 1995 and a leader in production design and 3D modelling services for the shipbuilding and offshore industries, we believe success is built by people who are passionate, curious, and committed to excellence. Proudly supporting major initiatives like Canada’s National Shipbuilding Strategy (NSS) and commercial projects across North America, we foster an environment where collaboration drives innovation, learning and development are continuous, and every team member plays a vital role in shaping our future.

 

ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

As a Controller, you’ll play a key role in ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and efficiency of Genoa’s financial operations. You will oversee the full accounting cycle, managing day-to-day financial, treasury, and accounting functions, while leading the development of annual budgets and financial reporting. With a strong emphasis on internal controls and operational excellence, you’ll support strategic decision-making and champion continuous improvement across financial practices.

title: Chief Executive Officer, Health PEI
description:
Chief Executive Officer
Health PEI
Location: Charlottetown, Prince Edward Island

Lead the transformation of health care for Islanders. Health PEI is seeking a visionary, collaborative, and results-oriented Chief Executive Officer to guide the organization through its next chapter.

The Company
Health PEI is the provincial health authority responsible for delivering publicly funded health services across Prince Edward Island. Guided by its mission of delivering high-quality, person-centered care to every Islander, Health PEI provides a broad continuum of services, including primary care, acute care, long-term care, mental health and addictions services, public health programs, and community-based care. Health PEI employers over 5000 dedicated staff with an operating budget of approximately $1.2 billion.

Health PEI's 2025–2028 Strategic Plan outlines an ambitious path forward focused on connecting every Islander to primary care, improving access to care that meets or exceeds national standards, advancing a One Island Health System approach, and building a more integrated and connected health system around the needs of Islanders. The organization is guided by the values of accountability, care, collaboration, and excellence. 

The Role

Reporting to the Board of Directors under the direction of the Minister of Health and Wellness, the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) provides strategic and operational leadership for Health PEI and is accountable for the overall performance of the

title: Chief Executive Officer
description:
Swept Technologies Inc. (Swept) is transforming how commercial cleaning businesses operate. Founded by industry operators who experienced the challenges of managing frontline teams firsthand, Swept is a leading workforce and operations platform that helps cleaning companies connect their people, streamline operations, and deliver exceptional service. Today, hundreds of organizations across North America rely on Swept's mobile-first technology to manage communication, tasks, quality assurance, training, and workforce performance.

Commercial cleaning represents a vital industry powered by resilient entrepreneurs and dedicated frontline workers. Swept exists to help these organizations operate more effectively, improve service quality, and grow sustainably. The company offers a rare opportunity to create meaningful impact through technology that directly improves the success of small and mid-sized businesses and the livelihoods of the people they employ.

The Opportunity

After successfully navigating a period of transformation, Swept has emerged as a profitable, EBITDA-positive, self-financing SaaS business with a strong product and significant growth potential. The next chapter is focused on accelerating growth, enhancing customer retention, expanding product innovation, and increasing enterprise value.

Swept is now seeking their next Chief Executive Officer. Reporting to the Board, the CEO will lead Swept through its next phase of value creation. Building on the organizatio

title: Chief Financial Officer
location: Happy Valley Goose Bay, Newfoundland and Labrador, CA
description:
Chief Financial Officer, Nunatsiavut Group of Companies
Happy Valley-Goose Bay, NL

Join Nunatsiavut Group of Companies (NGC) as Chief Financial Officer and play a key role in supporting the financial strength and day-to-day success of a diverse, community-focused organization. 

The Company

​​​​​​​The Nunatsiavut Group of Companies (NGC), based in Labrador, is dedicated to generating sustainable economic value for Nunatsiavut Beneficiaries. Operating under the Labrador Inuit Capital Strategy Trust (LICST), NGC is a leader across sectors including marine services, aviation, logistics, construction, mining, and real estate. Since 2011, NGC has continued to build on its foundation as Nunatsiavut’s economic development corporation, with a focus on steady growth, sound financial management, and long-term community benefit. 

The Role

​​​​​​​Reporting to the President & CEO and working closely with the Chief Operating Officer, the CFO will play a key role in ensuring strong financial oversight across a diverse group of operating companies. This role is well suited to someone who enjoys a hands-on environment and takes pride in building strong financial processes, producing reliable reporting, and supporting day-to-day business operations. The successful candidate will contribute to a stable and well-run organization while helping ensure financial discipline and accountability across its business units. 

Key areas of oversight and responsibility:

	
	Financial Oversight and Plan

title: Vice President, Clinical and Service Excellence
description:
Youthdale Treatment Centres (Youthdale) is a non-profit charitable organization located in Toronto that has been providing compassionate, professional, and collaborative care to children, youth, emerging adults, and families for over 50 years. Accredited with Exemplary Standing by Accreditation Canada, Youthdale serves approximately 3,500 clients each year through a comprehensive range of services, including crisis intervention, live-in treatment, secure treatment, day treatment, outpatient services, and community-based supports.

With a strong commitment to equity, diversity, inclusion, and belonging, Youthdale delivers trauma-informed, evidence-based, and culturally responsive care aimed at helping young people achieve resilience, stability, and hope. As an organization dedicated to advancing mental health equity, Youthdale collaborates closely with hospitals, schools, community agencies, and government partners to ensure children, youth, and their families have access to the highest quality of care. 

Youthdale is seeking a Vice President, Clinical and Service Excellence (Vice President) who plays a crucial role in shaping organizational strategy, strengthening partnerships within the system, advancing innovation, and ensuring Youthdale remains responsive to emerging trends and priorities in Ontario's child and youth mental health sector. Reporting to the President and CEO and serving on the Senior Leadership Team, the Vice President provides strategic leadership for Youth

title: Chief Operating Officer
description:
Lead Essential Services. Drive Operational Excellence. Enhance Community Impact.

The City of Saint John is Canada's oldest incorporated city and one of Atlantic Canada's most historic and evolving municipalities. Nestled on the Bay of Fundy, Saint John is a vibrant urban centre with a rich heritage, strong industrial foundation, growing multicultural population, and a bold vision for the future.

Guided by its mission to "Grow the City. Serve the City. Be the city of choice," the City is committed to delivering exceptional services, fostering growth and inclusion, and creating a thriving community for residents and businesses alike. The City employs more than 600 permanent staff who work together across six service areas to provide essential municipal services and support continued community growth.

As part of a newly designed executive leadership structure, the City is creating greater alignment across services and strengthening its ability to deliver high-quality, sustainable, and efficient municipal operations for the benefit of residents and businesses.

The Opportunity

The City of Saint John is seeking an accomplished operational leader to serve as its next Chief Operating Officer (COO).

Reporting directly to the Chief Administrative Officer and serving as a key member of the executive leadership team, the COO will provide strategic and operational leadership for the City's core service delivery functions, including Public Works, Water, Wastewater and Stormwater Serv

title: Chief Corporate Officer
description:
Lead Change. Build Capability. Drive Results.

The City of Saint John is Canada's oldest incorporated city and one of Atlantic Canada's most historic and evolving municipalities. Nestled on the Bay of Fundy, Saint John is a vibrant urban centre with a rich heritage, strong industrial foundation, growing multicultural population, and a bold vision for the future. Guided by its mission to "Grow the City. Serve the City. Be the city of choice," the City is committed to delivering exceptional services, fostering growth and inclusion, and creating a thriving community for residents and businesses alike.

The City employs more than 600 permanent staff who work together across six service areas to provide essential municipal services and support continued community growth.

As part of an exciting evolution of its executive leadership structure, the City of Saint John is strengthening organizational alignment, accountability, and performance to build a more collaborative, integrated, and outcomes-focused organization capable of meeting the needs of a growing community.

The Opportunity

The City of Saint John is seeking a strategic and transformational leader to serve as its next Chief Corporate Officer (CCO). Reporting directly to the Chief Administrative Officer and serving as a key member of the executive leadership team, the CCO will provide leadership for the City's corporate services portfolio, including Human Resources, Communications, Information Technology, Organizational Pe

title: Chief Financial Offier
description:
NL2 Capital Inc. is a publicly listed company on the TSX Venture Exchange that was established to identify and complete a business combination with a high-quality operating platform. Through its transformative Qualifying Transaction with the Talon Group, NL2 is evolving from a capital pool company into a fully operating public entity, expected to operate as Talon Diversified Holdings Inc. upon closing.

The Talon Group is a Newfoundland and Labrador-based industrial services platform comprised of established operating businesses including Talon Energy Services, Basil Fearn (93) Limited, and Trinity Roofing. Together, these companies serve industrial, commercial, infrastructure, marine, energy, mining, transportation, public sector, and construction markets throughout Atlantic Canada.

In a strong business position, the organization enters this next chapter with an established operating foundation, demonstrated profitability, and significant growth potential. By transitioning to a public company, Talon Diversified Holdings Inc. will gain enhanced access to capital, enabling it to pursue larger-scale industrial and infrastructure opportunities and accelerate its growth strategy through both organic expansion and strategic acquisitions. With strong fundamentals and exposure to sectors experiencing sustained investment and development, the organization is entering a period of considerable opportunity.

As a newly formed public company, Talon Diversified Holdings Inc. will combine

title: Chief Executive Officer
description:
Guided by a steadfast commitment to academic excellence, equity, inclusion, innovation, and strong community partnerships, the Kawartha Pine Ridge District School Board (KPR) focuses on delivering world‑class education and supporting the success, well‑being, and lifelong learning of every student. Serving approximately 35,000 learners across 89 schools in the Municipality of Clarington, Northumberland and Peterborough Counties, and the City of Quinte West/Murray Ward, KPR supports diverse rural and urban communities and is dedicated to fostering environments where all students can thrive.

The board is situated on the traditional territory of the Michi Saagiig Anishinaabeg people, serving the three Indigenous communities of Alderville First Nation, Curve Lake First Nation, and Hiawatha First Nation.

Today, the land on which the Kawartha Pine Ridge District School Board gathers is home to many First Nations, Métis and Inuit peoples and we acknowledge the contributions and accomplishments of all Indigenous people across Turtle Island, current and throughout history.

The Opportunity
Kawartha Pine Ridge District School Board is seeking a Chief Executive Officer (CEO), who will advance organizational performance, implement the Board’s strategic direction, and ensure the effective, transparent, and accountable operations of a large and complex publicly funded school system. 

Reporting to the Board of Trustees, the CEO serves as the Secretary of the Board, guiding organizational

Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently seeking a Electrical/Electronics Technician to join our team in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia.

Perform basic soldering and wire routing as required for assembly.
Handle and position small components, wires, and connectors with precision and care to prevent damage and ensure proper alignment.
Working with high voltages during electrical integration of components according to schematics, drawing and assembly.
Complete testing of various assemblies throughout the assembly process.
Conduct inspections of in-process and completed assemblies to ensure compliance with quality standards.
Perform basic soldering and wire routing as required for assembly.
Record assembly data, complete production checklists, and maintain accurate documentation of work performed.
Follow all safety procedures and wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) to maintain a safe working environment.
Notify supervisors of low stock or the need for replenishment of components, ensuring production timelines are met.

Summer Law Student & Articling Opportunities

At McInnes Cooper, we recognize that your professional development is an essential element of becoming a trusted lawyer. Our formal mentorship program will provide you with access to practical hands-on work while exposing you to the business aspects of a legal practice. Although we are considered a larger law firm on the East Coast, we organize ourselves into smaller teams to nurture collaboration and mentorship within our firm. This team model ensures you have the guidance necessary to build a successful legal career.

Do you want to be part of a mission driven organization shaping Canada’s innovation future? Do you thrive in collaborative environments where creativity, continuous improvement, and strategic thinking are valued? As Mitacs undergoes a multi-year enterprise transformation, we are building the capabilities, systems, and culture to deliver greater impact at scale. We are seeking a highly experienced Program Manager (1-year contract) to lead the orchestration and delivery of programs under the Transformation Management Office (TMO) Portfolio, including, but not limited to, Mitacs’ Enterprise Transformation (MET).

Reporting to the Director, Enterprise Transformation, this role is accountable for ensuring alignment, integration, and delivery across multiple corporate initiatives.

title: Program Manager / Gestionnaire de programme
description:
Vous souhaitez faire partie d’une organisation propulsée par sa mission qui façonne le futur de l’innovation au Canada? Vous aimez évoluer dans des milieux collaboratifs où la créativité, l’amélioration continue et la pensée stratégique sont valorisées? Êtes‑vous une personne motivée par des responsabilités qui contribuent à accroître la compétitivité du Canada et à produire des résultats concrets pour les communautés et les entreprises? 

Pour développer la capacité d’innovation du Canada, à Mitacs, nous misons sur le talent. Nous établissons des liens entre les chercheurs et chercheuses et les organisations dans toutes les régions du pays pour aider à résoudre des défis pressants et accélérer la croissance économique. En facilitant la collaboration entre le secteur privé et le milieu postsecondaire et en appuyant le perfectionnement de talents hautement qualifiés, nous contribuons à une économique canadienne résiliente et capable de relever les défis de demain.

Guidé·es par nos valeurs, impact, intégrité, collaboration, excellence et talent, nous collaborons avec des partenaires de partout au pays pour faire avancer la recherche, la commercialisation et l’innovation. 

Description du poste :
Nous sommes à la recherche d’un ou d’une gestionnaire de programme pour diriger la planification et la mise en œuvre de programmes du Bureau de gestion de la transformation, y compris, sans s’y limiter, la transformation organisationnelle de Mitacs (MET).

GET STARTED

CAREERS

For developers,
by developers.

Helping companies move fast by removing their bottlenecks.

ABOUT PINGRAM
CONTACT US
SEC. 01
OPEN ROLES
We're hiring

FREELANCE / PART-TIME

Sponsored OSS Developer

If you would like to write and release an SDK in a programming language or framework we don't yet support, we can sponsor your work.

Assuming you follow our SDK guidelines, we'll gladly pay you a market-competitive day rate for your development time.

APPLY NOW
SEC. 02
PHILOSOPHY
Built for perpetuity

Dev tools are different.

You can't "move fast and break things" if the dev tools you rely on do the same: raise prices as they experiment, shut down features when they pivot, or change org structure without warning.

So we decided to build the opposite:

A stable business without external funding
A strong focus on quality and affordability
Unit economics strong enough to do this forever

At our core, we are builders passionate about building a great product.

SEC. 03
VALUES
Product, people, perpetuity
01
Product

Build something you'd be proud of. Build it in the image of your beautiful mind.

02
People

The real treasure is the developers you meet along the way. Have some fun.

03
Perpetuity

Built to last. One day your grandchildren's notifications will go through here.

SEC. 04
CULTURE
Working at Pingram
01
Developers' Club

Except for legal and finance, everyone at Pingram has an extensive development background.
