The Office of the Auditor General values diversity in the workplace and is an equal-opportunity employer.

Disability-related accommodations and alternate formats related to competitions are available on request by contacting 709-725-3351.

Preference will be given to applicants who are Canadian citizens or permanent residents of Canada, or those who are legally entitled to work in Canada.

Competitions posted may be used to fill future similar vacancies with the Office of the Auditor General.

How to Apply:

Applications must be received on or before the closing date stated for a job posting. It is the responsibility of the applicant to submit an application that demonstrates the required merit criteria. All applications must contain accurate contact information, including current mailing address, email address and phone number. All information submitted as part of an application must be factual, complete and current to the date of submission. Applicants must submit by email to careers@oag.nl.ca

Civilian Opportunities

The RNC has roughly 100 civilian employees who play an important role in the delivery of police services every day. Civilians are employed in a wide variety of disciplines including finance, administration, planning, communication, information management and technology, legal services, as well as asset and facilities management.

Civilian employment opportunities available within the RNC are advertised through the Public Service Commission, Government of Newfoundland and Labrador. Visit https://www.hiring.gov.nl.ca to view current job postings.

With a network of 94 sites, Newfoundland and Labrador Public Libraries provides library services throughout the province of Newfoundland and Labrador.  Embracing the diversity of our patrons and their cultures, we offer resources that include traditional library services as well as modern innovative programs, spaces, and technologies.

If you are a community-minded individual who enjoys helping people of all ages, we want you to join our team!

Account Manager
Account Manager

About Omega 365 

At Omega 365 Canada, we empower teams to deliver complex projects efficiently and effectively across industries. As part of the global Omega 365 group, our innovative software solutions and expert consultancy services support clients in project management, engineering, and digital transformation. From energy and infrastructure to technology and manufacturing, Omega 365 partners with leading organizations to optimize workflows, enhance productivity, and drive successful outcomes.


Omega 365 is a modern, industry-leading, ISO-certified, cloud-native technology platform with built-in AI capabilities that support critical processes across organizations and projects. Our clients range from oil and gas, energy, renewables, IT, mining, construction, infrastructure, and healthcare.

At Omega 365, we value collaboration, innovation, and a passion for client success. We foster a supportive, dynamic environment where team members are empowered to contribute their ideas and grow together.




Role Overview

As an Account Manager at Omega 365, you’ll be the pivotal link between our organization and our clients, ensuring their ongoing satisfaction and successful use of our solutions. You will manage key client relationships, oversee service delivery, and proactively identify opportunities to expand our service

Cook – Dredging Operations
Cook – Dredging Operations

Overview:

The Dredging Operations team is based at the Support facility on the East Bank of Demerara and works across various locations along the Demerara River, carrying out dredging activities that support safe marine access and operational needs. The Cook will be responsible for preparing meals and providing catering support for personnel working on these projects.

Responsibilities:

Prepare and serve scheduled meals in a timely manner.

Plan weekly menus that meet nutritional guidelines and crew preferences.

Maintain cleanliness of facilities.

Ensure proper food handling, storage, and hygiene practices are followed.

Monitor inventory levels and coordinate with supply personnel to ensure timely restocking.

Minimize food waste through proper portioning, storage, and menu planning.

Maintain food logs & cleaning schedules.

Comply with maritime HSE regulations.

Communicate with supervisors and crew to address special dietary needs or meal feedback.

Any other duty that may be assigned to you by your supervisor that is within your scope of work.

Requirements:

Minimum 2–3 years’ experience as a Cook in a marine, offshore, or industrial setting.

Certification in Food Handling, Preparation, and Hygiene.

Knowledge of safe kitchen practices and basic nutrition.

Electrician (Marine)
Electrician (Marine)

Responsibilities:

Install conduits to bulkheads, thread wires, and connect to terminals such as connection boxes, circuit breakers, and switch panels.

Construct instrument panels according to specifications using hand and power tools and technical drawings.

Install, maintain, and troubleshoot electrical systems, wiring, fixtures, and control devices aboard vessels.

Perform preventive and predictive maintenance and diagnose faults using test equipment and hand tools.

Strip insulation, solder wires to terminals, and conduct insulation resistance measurements on cables.

Repair and maintain generators, switchboards, motors, circuit breakers, distribution panels, alarm systems, and lighting circuits.

Test electrical characteristics including voltage, resistance, and phase angle using appropriate instruments.

Perform minor repairs on engines, including plumbing, hydraulic, steering systems, and replacement of worn components.

Maintain accurate service records, repair documentation, and electrical system maintenance histories.

Inspect systems for hazards and compliance, supervise installations/modifications, and troubleshoot onboard electronic equipment (radio, radar, sonar, etc.).

Diploma or Certificate in Electrical Engineering or Marine Electrical Systems.

Konsulent til digitalisering og KI- Ukom
Konsulent til digitalisering og KI- Ukom

Omega 365 Consulting er en ledende leverandør av dyktig prosjektpersonell til aktører innen olje, gass, fornybar energi og infrastruktur. Vi tilbyr et bredt utvalg av konsulentoppdrag, inkludert mange av de mest spennende prosjektene i Norge og i utlandet.




Detaljer:

Varighet: 01.09.2026 - 31.12.2026
Lokasjon: Stavanger
Opsjoner: 6 + 6 måneder
Søknadsfrist: 04.08.2026, kl 12:00




Beskrivelse av oppdraget

Statens undersøkelseskommisjon for helse- og omsorgstjenesten – er en uavhengig statlig etat under Helse- og omsorgsdepartementet, etablert i 2018. Ukom har som formål å bedre pasient- og brukersikkerheten i helse- og omsorgstjenesten gjennom å undersøke alvorlige hendelser og forhold i tjenestene. Kommisjonen holder til i Stavanger.

Ukom står i et teknologisk skifte der KI vil påvirke hvordan vi jobber. For å bruke dette trygt og strategisk, trenger vi en dedikert ressurs som kan støtte ledergruppen og drive utviklingen.

Ukom har de siste årene modernisert IT plattformen, og har nylig innført Copilot. Ledergruppen har behov for en ressurs som kan gi struktur og innsikt og som har gjennomføringsevne. Vi ser etter en ressurs som forstår både mulighetene og begrensningene i bruken av KI.

Du skal bistå ledergruppen og ta et særskilt ansvar for hvordan Ukom ta

Through innovation, operational excellence, and a commitment to public service, the NSLC continues to modernize its business and technology landscape to better serve Nova Scotians. As part of this transformation, NSLC is embarking on a significant SAP modernization initiative and is seeking a Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner (BPO) to help shape the future of enterprise reporting and analytics. This two-year term opportunity offers the chance to play a pivotal role in one of the organization's most strategic transformation programs, ensuring data, reporting, and business intelligence capabilities are designed to support informed decision-making across the enterprise. Reporting to the Director, Insights & Strategy, this role will serve as the primary business owner for reporting strategy, analytics alignment, and enterprise data definitions while partnering closely with business leaders, project teams, and technology stakeholders throughout the SAP implementation. This immersive approach equips students with the knowledge, confidence, and real-world skills required to thrive in modern dental practices.  Guided by a collaborative, student-centred culture, Keyin College is committed to educational excellence while fostering the success of its students, faculty, and staff. Combining deep technical expertise with industry knowledge, InnovMarine partners with organizations to modernize operations, improve decision-making, and unlock greater value through digital transformation and emerging technologies. As InnovMarine continues to expand across Atlantic Canada, they are seeking a Senior Sales Engineer to help grow their presence within the maritime and defence industries. This unique opportunity blends technical pre-sales expertise with consultative enterprise sales, making it ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex business challenges into practical technology solutions. title: Social Worker
description: Venor is proud to partner with Oromocto First Nation Child and Family Services in their search for a Social Worker to join their team. Oromocto First Nation Child and Family Services (OCFS) is a designated child and family services agency dedicated to serving the children and families of Oromocto First Nation. Grounded in community, culture, and collaboration, OCFS works to keep children safe, strengthen families, and ensure children remain connected to their identity, traditions, and support systems. Unlike larger agencies, OCFS serves a single community, allowing for smaller caseloads and a more personalized approach to care. Team members work closely together, creating an environment where social workers can devote meaningful time and attention to each child and family while receiving strong organizational support. Reporting to the Director of OCFS, the Social Worker will play a critical role in promoting the safety, wellbeing, and long-term success of children and families within the community. This is a full-time, on-site opportunity based in Oromocto First Nation, New Brunswick, with local travel required and participation in a rotating after-hours on-call schedule. What you'll be doing Conduct child protection investigations, risk assessments, and safety assessments to support the wellbeing of children and families. Develop and manage case plans focused on family preservation, reunification, and kinship or community-based care. Support families navigating complex chall

title: Branch Manager
description: Our client, Graybar is seeking a dynamic and results-driven Branch Manager to lead and grow the branch operations in Kentville, Nova Scotia. This role is ideal for a strategic leader who thrives on developing business opportunities, driving operational excellence, and building high-performing teams. Reporting to the Regional Vice President, the Branch Manager, you will have full accountability for branch performance—balancing sales growth, customer satisfaction, financial management, and employee development—while acting as a key ambassador of the company in the local market. What You’ll Do Leadership & Strategy Provide strategic direction and lead the execution of branch initiatives aligned with company objectives. Champion a culture of accountability, integrity, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Act as a role model by consistently following company policies, procedures, and values. Sales & Customer Growth Maintain and grow sales volume while strengthening customer relationships. Identify and develop new business opportunities, products, and customers. Build strong relationships with customers and suppliers through meetings, calls, and industry events. Ensure all reasonable customer requests are handled promptly and professionally. Operations & Financial Management Oversee pricing strategies, inventory management, and inventory turns. Manage accounts receivable, credit risks, and cost controls. Prepare and manage annual, monthly, and weekly operational plans and bud

title: Manager of Corporate Accounting
description: Venor is proud to partner with Graybar Canada on their search for a Manager of Corporate Accounting to join their head office team in Halifax, NS. Graybar Canada is a leader in the distribution of high-quality electrical, automation, and telecommunications products, supporting these operations through supply chain management and logistics services. Graybar Canada operates more than 35 branches across Canada. As one of Canada's largest employee-owned companies, Graybar Canada has the power and stability of a big corporation and the integrity and drive of a neighborhood business. What You’ll Be Doing The Manager of Corporate Accounting will support the Director of Accounting and VP, Finance in managing the day-to-day operations of the finance function for the organization. This is a key leadership role within the finance team, ensuring financial statements are accurate, compliant with accounting standards, and delivered on time. We're looking for a collaborative, hands-on professional who enjoys being involved in the details, isn't afraid to roll up their sleeves, and is eager to learn. Success in this role will come from a combination of technical capability, initiative, adaptability, and a genuine desire to contribute and gain new skills. Key Responsibilities Financial Reporting & Close: Support the complete accounting cycle: journal entries, accruals, prepayments, depreciation, account reconciliations & compiling working papers Support the Controller with monthly, quarterly,

title: Office Administrator & Bookkeeper
description: Venor is proud to partner with Archibald Drilling & Blasting in their search for an Office Administrator & Bookkeeper to join their team in Truro, Nova Scotia. Archibald Drilling & Blasting is a leading provider of drilling and blasting services, with more than 40 years of experience delivering safe, innovative, and reliable solutions across quarry operations, open-pit mining, civil construction, and marine projects. Known for its commitment to safety, technical excellence, and continuous improvement, Archibald supports some of the most complex and demanding projects throughout Atlantic Canada and beyond. As the company continues to grow, Archibald is seeking an organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Office Administrator & Bookkeeper to support day-to-day office operations and financial administration. This is a critical role within the organization, ideal for someone who enjoys variety in their work and thrives in a small business environment where they can make a meaningful impact. Reporting directly to leadership, the successful candidate will oversee office administration while managing core accounting and bookkeeping functions. This position offers the opportunity to help shape and improve internal processes as the company continues to evolve. What You'll Be Doing Manage the day-to-day administrative operations of the office Process payroll accurately and in a timely manner Manage accounts payable and accounts receivable functions Perform general bookkeeping duties an

title: Labour Relations Specialist
description: Venor is proud to partner with J.D. Irving, Limited in their search for a new Labour Relations Specialist to join their Human Resources team. J.D. Irving, Limited (JDI) is a premier North American forestry and operations leader. What began in 1882 as a regional, family-owned enterprise has grown into a dynamic, coast-to-coast group of operating divisions with over 140 years of success. Recognized for building a highly efficient, vertically integrated supply chain, J.D. Irving, Limited delivers high-quality lumber, pulp and paper products, and sustainable forest management across Eastern Canada and the United States. At Grand Lake Timber Sawmill, Chipman, NB, JDI is proud of their strong legacy in sustainable forestry and high-quality wood products. Reporting to the HR Manager, the Labour Relations Specialist will play a key role in supporting positive union-management relations, ensuring compliance with collective agreements, and promoting a culture of fairness and accountability. They will support the business by interpreting and administering the collective agreement, maintaining a productive relationship with the Union, managing the grievance process, providing training and development to supervisors on company policies and labour relations best practices, assisting with progressive discipline practices, resolving complex labour relations issues and conducting workplace investigations in conjunction with Industrial Security Ltd. The ideal candidate has a strong background

title: EDI and API Integration Analyst
description: Venor is proud to partner with Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics in their search for an EDI and API Integration Analyst to join their team. Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is a premier North American transportation and logistics leader. What began in 1964 as a regional, family-owned petroleum delivery operation in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has grown into a coast-to-coast group of companies with over 60 years of success. Recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and an industry Employer of Choice, Seaboard Armour delivers safe, reliable, and innovative bulk transport, freight forwarding, and supply chain solutions across Canada and the United States. As part of their growing Data and Integration team,  the EDI and API Integration Analyst is responsible for the operation, support, documentation, monitoring, onboarding, and continuous improvement of customer and partner data integrations across EDI, API, and file-based exchange methods. This role ensures integration processes are reliable, well-documented, supportable, and aligned to business needs across transportation and logistics operations. As valued members of the expanding Data and Integration team, EDI and API Integration Analysts, along with current team of data integration professionals, collectively manage, support, document, monitor, onboard, and continuously enhance customer and partner data integrations across EDI, API, and file-based exchange methods. Together, the data integrati

title: Power BI Report Developer
description: Venor is proud to partner with Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics in their search for a Power BI Report Developer to join their team. Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is a premier North American transportation and logistics leader. What began in 1964 as a regional, family-owned petroleum delivery operation in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has grown into a coast-to-coast group of companies with over 60 years of success. Recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and an industry Employer of Choice, Seaboard Armour delivers safe, reliable, and innovative bulk transport, freight forwarding, and supply chain solutions across Canada and the United States. As part of their growing Data and Integration team, Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is seeking a Power BI Report Developer to design, develop, validate, and support trusted Power BI reports, dashboards, datasets, and analytics solutions across transportation, logistics, dispatch, billing, finance, safety, maintenance, customer service, and executive performance management. With a strong focus on report quality, performance, documentation, user acceptance testing, and adoption of standardized reporting practices, this role helps improve operational visibility, reduce manual reporting effort, and build a more mature analytics foundation for the business. The BI Report Developer is responsible for designing, developing, testing, validating, deploying, and supporting business intelligence repor

title: Marketing Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with Fidelis Law in their search for a Marketing Coordinator to join their team in Moncton, NB Fidelis Law is a dedicated personal injury firm with offices in Moncton and Edmundston, New Brunswick. The firm is committed to providing exceptional legal representation to personal injury victims while delivering personalized, compassionate service that ensures clients’ rights are protected and their voices are heard. As Fidelis Law continues to grow, they are seeking a Marketing Coordinator to support and execute marketing initiatives that strengthen brand awareness, enhance community presence, and support business development objectives. Reporting to the Partners of the firm, the Marketing Coordinator will play a key role in coordinating campaigns, creating engaging content, and supporting the day-to-day marketing activities that promote the firm's services and values. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative and organized marketing professional who enjoys working in a collaborative environment and contributing to meaningful work that positively impacts clients and communities across New Brunswick. What you'll be doing: Support the planning, coordination, and execution of marketing campaigns across digital, print, social media, and community engagement channels. Assist in establishing marketing plans and objectives that align with the firm's business initiatives and growth strategies. Create and manage content for social media platforms, website u

title: Vice President of Finance
description: Venor is excited to partner with the Nexco Group on their search for a Vice President of Finance to join their team based in Dartmouth, NS. Nexco Group is a proudly Nova Scotian, integrated group of companies delivering trusted solutions across civil construction, traffic control, and infrastructure services. Through its divisions, Nexco Construction, Nexco Traffic Control, and Nexco Environmental, the company plays a vital role in building and maintaining the infrastructure that supports communities across Atlantic Canada. With expertise spanning mass excavation, site development, demolition, road building, highway structures and bridges, retaining walls, and traffic management, Nexco Group is known for its commitment to safety, quality, reliability, and collaboration. Driven by a passion for excellence and a people-first approach, Nexco partners with clients to successfully deliver projects that contribute to Atlantic Canada's continued growth and development. About the Role: We are seeking a highly strategic Vice President of Finance to provide executive-level oversight for the Nexco Group. As the ultimate owner of our financial vision, you will drive big-picture planning, manage complex cash flow and risk, and optimize internal controls to ensure sustainable, profitable growth across Nexco and a subsidiary of companies. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Financial Leadership & Planning Serve as the primary financial visionary for the Nexco Group, aligning financial strategie

title: Supply Chain Manager
description: SureShot Solutions designs, manufactures, and markets the most innovative dispensers of beverages and beverage add-ins, quenching the thirst of anyone looking for a better way to serve refreshments. With many industry firsts, they are passionate about identifying customer needs, and are tenacious about providing breakthrough solutions to address them. Over the years, they have proudly collaborated with top quick-serve restaurant chains and convenience retailers worldwide to design, engineer, manufacture, and market dispensers for beverages and beverage enhancers. At the heart of their success lies a deep-rooted commitment to excellence, where innovation meets unparalleled craftsmanship. Adaptability to change and a commitment to ensuring a safe workplace while delivering top-notch quality results are qualities SureShot holds in high regard. On behalf of SureShot, we are recruiting a Supply Chain Manager to lead key functional areas including procurement, inventory performance management, planning, and scheduling, while providing direct leadership to the Logistics Lead & Scheduler and the Purchasing Agent. Are you a strategic and systems-savvy supply chain professional who thrives on optimizing operations, leading teams, and delivering results? Reporting directly to the Vice President of Finance and Administration, this role is a critical member of the Supply Chain Management (SCM) Team and plays a central role in driving operational efficiency and supplier performance. What Y

title: Bilingual Senior Manager, Operations – New Brunswick
description: Venor is proud to partner with Circular Materials in their search for a Bilingual Senior Manager, Operations – New Brunswick to join their team. Circular Materials is a national producer-led not for profit organization that supports producers in meeting their obligations under extended producer responsibility regulations across Canada. They are committed to building efficient and effective recycling systems to minimize waste and ensure materials are reused again and again. Through the support of their founders and member producers, Circular Materials is implementing a national harmonized approach focused on improving recovery rates, meeting the needs and expectations of consumers, and protecting the natural environment. Reporting to the Managing Director, Atlantic, the Senior Manager, Operations – NB will play a pivotal leadership role in overseeing New Brunswick’s recycling common collection and post-collection systems under Provincial EPR legislation. This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of recycling in Atlantic Canada, build meaningful stakeholder relationships, and lead a team driving real environmental impact. What You’ll Be Doing Collection & Post-Collection Network Development Develop subject matter expertise on the regulatory requirements associated with the Stewardship Plan and the NB Designated Materials Packaging and Paper Regulations. Manage the PPP recycling common collection system and post-collection system in NB, in alignment with the program plan

title: Controller
description: Venor is excited to partner with Nexco Group on their search for a Controller to join their team based in Dartmouth, NS. Nexco Group is a proudly Nova Scotian, integrated group of companies delivering trusted solutions across civil construction, traffic control, and infrastructure services. Through its divisions, Nexco Construction, Nexco Traffic Control, and Nexco Environmental, the company plays a vital role in building and maintaining the infrastructure that supports communities across Nova Scotia. With expertise spanning mass excavation, site development, demolition, road building, highway structures and bridges, retaining walls, and traffic management, Nexco Group is known for its commitment to safety, quality, reliability, and collaboration. Driven by a passion for excellence and a people-first approach, Nexco partners with clients to successfully deliver projects that contribute to Nova Scotia's continued growth and development. About the Role: As the organization continues to experience significant growth, Nexco Group is seeking an experienced Controller to join its leadership team. Reporting directly to the VP of Finance, the Controller will play a critical role in overseeing the day-to-day accounting operations of the organization while providing financial insight to support strategic decision-making. As the Controller, you will work closely with operational leaders to ensure project financial performance is accurately monitored and reported while helping the compan

title: Operations Specialist
description: Venor is proud to partner with Circular Materials in their search for an Operations Specialist to join their team based in Halifax, Nova Scotia. The position is remote; however will involve frequent travel to a Material Recovery Facility located in Halifax. This is an exciting opportunity to be part of a mission-driven organization at the forefront of modernizing recycling in the region.   Circular Materials is a national producer-led not-for-profit organization that supports producers in meeting their obligations under extended producer responsibility regulations across Canada. They are committed to building efficient and effective recycling systems to minimize waste and ensure materials are reused again and again. Through the support of their founders and member producers, Circular Materials is implementing a national harmonized approach focused on improving recovery rates, meeting the needs and expectations of consumers, and protecting the natural environment.  The organization is growing its team to support these critical operations. This role is primarily analytical in nature, with a strong focus on the post-collection processes. The Operations Specialist will spend most of their time at a new, highly automated Material Recovery Facility (MRF) currently under construction in Halifax — set to go live in June 2027 — monitoring operations, analyzing data, and ensuring performance standards are met.    What You'll Be Doing: Reporting to the Senior Manager, Transition – Atlan

Position Title: Research Consultant Quantum Computing
Department: ACENET

Salary Range: $68,959 to $79,568
Posting Date: June 9, 2026
Closing Date: Will remain open until filled

This is a full-time position with no fixed end date, subject to external funding.

ACENET is seeking a Research Consultant in Quantum Computing to assist academic and industry clients in understanding, evaluating, and applying emerging quantum computing technologies alongside advanced computing resources (HPC, AI, Cloud) available through ACENET and the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

This role is focused on enabling early exploration and responsible adoption of quantum computing, rather than hardware development or theoretical research. The successful candidate will have access to quantum computing platforms to support hands-on experimentation and applied projects.

ACENET’s central mission is to help researchers and innovators get their work done more efficiently by providing access to advanced computing resources and making those resources easier to use.

Location: St. John’s, NL or surrounding area
Compensation: Competitive salary with comprehensive benefits package
Company Description
Altomaxx is a leading-edge drone-service provider, offering inspection services, integrated solutions, and capacity building within a diverse range of industries. Through policy development and leadership, Altomaxx shapes the drone industry, setting a high standard for safety and operational procedures. Altomaxx is a rapidly growing company, thanks to its team of world-class technical experts, strategic partnerships, dedication to continuous improvement, and unwavering commitment to its clients. Headquartered in St. John’s NL Canada, Altomaxx serves an international client-base and deploys drone technologies in projects around the globe.

We employ individuals in many disciplines, including social work, accounting, therapy, office administration and human resources, with our largest employee group consisting of child and youth care workers. Our workforce is regularly expanding as our organization grows.

If you are enthusiastic, passionate, committed to excellence and willing to work in a Growth Culture, then Amal may be the place for you.

The vision of Amal Youth and Family Centre is to become a centre of hope, and a warm, welcoming, respectful and diverse organization in which children, youth and families from myriad backgrounds thrive and succeed together.

company: AXIS Career Services-Association for New Canadians
title: Settlement Health Coordinator
location: St John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description:
Association for New Canadians is a non-profit, community-based organization dedicated to the provision of settlement and integration services for immigrants and refugees. 
 
Job Title: Settlement Health Coordinator 
Location: St. John’s, NL 
Position Type: 1 full-time position until March 31, 2027, with possibility of extension pending budget availability and satisfactory performance    
Closing date: Until Filled 
 
Reporting to the Social Work & Health Services Manager, the Settlement Health Coordinator is responsible for the day-to-day supervision of the Settlement Health team and coordinating health-related services to clients. This role will ensure that clients have access to health services and appropriate support. The Settlement Health Coordinator will work closely with health service providers and lead the internal team for a seamless client experience. 
 
 
Roles and Responsibilities: 
 

Connect newcomers with health services, including primary care, mental health services, and specialized healthcare  


Liaise with healthcare providers and community organizations to ensure clients have access to culturally appropriate healthcare  


Conduct assessments to identify health needs of clients, including barriers to accessing healthcare services  


Provide individualized support and case management to clients requiring additional assistance with navigating the healthcare system  


Assist clients with completing health-related forms and documentation Build partnerships
via: AXIS Career Services-Association for New Canadians

JUNIOR MECHANICAL ENGINEER - C-CORE

At C-CORE, we are committed to investing in people and creating a dynamic and rewarding employment experience. We are seeking a Junior Mechanical Engineer to join our Oceans & Energy Team. This position is based in St. John’s, NL.

If you have a passion for complex design solutions, hands-on fabrication, and contributing to cutting-edge R&D projects from concept to commissioning, this opportunity may be for you. Are you enthusiastic about engineering, attentive to detail, and motivated to support a diverse team of professionals in a dynamic environment? If so, we want you on our team!

About C-CORE

C-CORE conducts R&D and provides research-based advisory services, delivering innovative engineering and technology solutions to national and international clients. For over 50 years, we have built a world-class team of professionals in Remote Sensing and Oceans & Energy. We are global leaders in Earth Observation and geospatial intelligence, with our flagship platform Coresight driving major contracts in defense, environmental monitoring, and advanced R&D.

Are you passionate about cutting-edge technology and ready to make your mark? Are you motivated to shape the future of thriving businesses through innovative solutions? If so, we want you to join our high-energy Sage Intacct Sales team as a Customer Success Manager (Bilingual).

At Vision33, we are forward thinkers. We bring together best-in-class cloud technology and the most experienced people in the industry to deliver cloud-based ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) and business solutions that create transformative results for our clients.  

Due to continued growth, we are seeking a Customer Success Manager (Bilingual)with a successful background in managing customer relationships and driving internal sales opportunities.   

You will be at the heart of nurturing and growing our client relationships. You will work as a consultative partner for our clients, understanding key priorities and implementing strategies and business solutions for their success.  

First Point of Contact for our Clients: You will be the supportive, consultative, and advisory friendly face for assigned clients and the first stop for business solution inquiries and questions. 


Develop and Execute Customer Management Plans: You will work the client and focus on Key Accounts and their Affiliates.

About Wedgwood Insurance

At Wedgwood Insurance, our core values of We Care, We Lead, We Improve, and We Deliver are reflected in everything we do. It’s how we’ve become the most trusted insurance broker in Newfoundland & Labrador and have been part of our foundation for over 45 years. We invest in our team by promoting personal and professional development, providing amazing benefits, and fostering a culture of respect and pride in our work. And it shows as we’ve been named one of the Best Places to Work in Atlantic Canada by Atlantic Business Magazine!

We are looking for a System Specialist to join our St. John’s office.

Our Career Opportunity – System Specialist

This isn’t a support desk role. As Systems Specialist, you are the person who keeps the engine running. You’ll own the operation, maintenance, and optimization of our brokerage management system – primarily Applied EPIC – along with the supporting platforms our team depends on every day.

You’ll be the go-to for everything from system configuration and data integrity to user support, report building, and troubleshooting. When something goes wrong, staff know who to

title: FastTrackHER Pilot
description:
Skip to content
EMPLOYER SERVICES
CAREER & YOUTH SERVICES
ABOUT
NEWS & EVENTS
CONTACT
CAREER AND YOUTH SERVICES

Equiforce provides innovative, responsive, and industry-informed workforce development initiatives that build equitable pathways into the industries shaping Newfoundland and Labrador’s future and connect women and gender-diverse individuals with opportunity.

CAREER PROGRAMS
FASTTRACKHER

An innovative workforce readiness initiative aimed at preparing women for high-demand industries. FastTrackHER is a new pilot in development for 2026. Short, intensive, and industry-informed, it creates pathways into underrepresented fields through sector connections, relevant certifications, and direct insight into emerging industry opportunities. The first pilot focuses on Newfoundland and Labrador’s construction sector in the Avalon region, with future iterations expanding to other industries across the province.

PATHWAYS TO PLACE

A province-wide pilot launching in 2026 that connects newcomer and immigrant women with eight-week, paid, work-integrated learning placements in local organizations. Participants gain workplace readiness skills, valuable Canadian experience, professional networks, and wraparound supports that promote success and belonging. Employers, in turn, strengthen their capacity through structured guidance that supports inclusive hiring, onboarding, and workplace practices.

For more information and to learn how to participate, click here.  

CAREER PATHWAYS

description: POSITION SUMMARY   Issue, open, and serve wine/champagne bottles. Answer guest questions or concerns regarding the origin, vintage, and style of various wines. Update and maintain wine list and MICROS system. Pair and suggest wines that will best complement menu items. Attend wine tastings and develop relationships with vendors. Request new wines and products. Create and update wine lists. Design and implement wine promotions and incentive programs. Monitor and replenish inventory of wine cellar, equipment, and glassware. Train and educate server and bartender staff. Conduct vintage and BIN number checks. Conduct staff wine tastings. Secure liquors, beers, wines, coolers, cabinets, and storage areas. Follow all state and local laws for serving alcohol responsibly. Maintain accurate spill sheet.   Follow all company and safety and security policies and procedures; report accidents, injuries, and unsafe work conditions to manager; and complete safety training and certifications. Ensure uniform and personal appearance are clean and professional, maintain confidentiality of proprietary information, and protect company assets. Welcome and acknowledge all guests according to company standards, anticipate and address guests’ service needs, assist individuals with disabilities, and thank guests with genuine appreciation. Office Clerk
Office Clerk | Moncton, NB



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

Our Moncton office has an immediate opening for an Office Clerk. Position is permanent, full-time.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Managing, ordering, and stocking of all office supplies;
Basic maintenance of photocopiers and the postage machine;
Light tidying of pantries and the staff kitchen;
Providing reception backup relief as needed;
Delivery of time sensitive documents to other firms, financial institutions and the Court as needed;
Assisting with special projects and/or administrative duties assigned by the office manager; and
Depositing outgoing mail at end of day.
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

Spoken and written proficiency in English is required; bilingualism is considered an asset;
1 year of experience working in an administrative role or professional services environment;
Must have their own vehicle for daily banking errands as needed (mileage is paid);
Above average proficiency in Microsoft Outlook and other applications;

WHO WE ARE:
Mariner innovates always and everywhere, turning novel ideas into practical solutions. From products and services to venture creation, we build better futures powered by people. We partner with people embracing change, leveraging data, cybersecurity, cloud, and change management to drive successful business outcomes that matter to our customers.

Mariners build for change. Like SHIFT Energy, our energy management and decarbonization company. Or East Valley, created to scale social economic impact in our communities through investment and mentorship of emerging change-makers and their companies.

Mariner’s purpose has always been to be a great, everlasting technology company and the core values that guide us every day are:

OUR VALUES

We care for our team.
We care about the impact we have on our community.
We serve by solving complex problems.
We grow because growth fuels opportunities.
We innovate always and everywhere.
We build a business that lasts.

We are seeking a talented and experienced Business Analyst to join our Mariner Innovations team full-time to help in our planned growth, and contract opportunities for our clients in Atlantic Canada. CO-OP ENGINEERING

Are you ready to apply your engineering knowledge in a real-world environment and gain hands-on experience supporting the electrical distribution system that powers communities across Nova Scotia?

We’re looking for an Electrical Engineering Co-op Student to join our Regional Engineering team in Sackville for the Fall term. This is an exciting opportunity to work alongside experienced engineers and technical professionals, contributing to projects that support the safe, reliable, and efficient operation of our distribution network.

As a member of our team, you’ll gain practical engineering experience, develop your technical skills, and build a deeper understanding of utility operations while making meaningful contributions to ongoing projects and initiatives.

Support the operation, planning, and design of the electrical distribution network.
Review customer servicing submissions and fault-level requests.
Update underground drawing records and support markup of new revisions.
Gather and analyze field data related to distribution assets.
Develop an understanding of electrical overcurrent and protective devices used on the distribution network.
Collaborate with engineers, technical staff, and operational teams on a variety of projects and initiatives.

We are seeking a Senior Web Developer with strong expertise in React to join our CGI offices in Atlantic Canada. Design, develop, and maintain modern web applications using React and other JavaScript frameworks
Build scalable and maintainable front-end architectures, including single-page applications (SPAs)

Collaborate closely with Product, Design, and engineering teams to deliver innovative and user-centric features

 Job Posts Content Engineer (Part-Time)

CONTENT ENGINEER (PART-TIME)

Foundation is an AI visibility marketing agency that drives pipeline growth for mid-market and enterprise B2B tech and SaaS companies. We help our clients generate pipeline through content engineered to distribute across LLMs, search, social, communities like Reddit, and every channel their buyers actually use.

The Business Case
Writers spend 2–3 hours on every brief. Editors spend 15–30 minutes reviewing and approving each one. AirOps automates brief creation. A writer submits a keyword, the system generates a structured brief, an editor reviews in 15 minutes or less. That 100–150 hours goes back to drafting. The Content Engineer owns the system that makes this work at scale.

Why This Role Exists
AirOps is currently being built and expanded across three pilot clients. As it scales to more clients and content types, it needs a dedicated owner — someone who designs, maintains, and grows the workflows. The workflow logic, prompt architecture, and brand kit structure require focused ownership. Adding this to a writer or editor’s existing workload cancels out the time savings the system creates.

What the Role Does

Design and maintain AirOps content workflows across all content types
Create and configure brand kits during client onboarding; maintain the workflow that keeps them synced when editors update writer’s guides — Co

title: Manager – Governance, Risk and Compliance Services (Insurance)
description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference. Our Governance, Risk and Compliance Services (GRCS) professionals provide a range of assurance and advisory services to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of internal audit functions, enterprise risk management programs, third-party relationships, regulatory compliance, governance and sustainability initiatives. We analyze and assess an organization’s business strategies and related business processes and systems to help management understand and address business objectives, requirements, risks, controls, and improvement opportunities. Our professionals bring both deep technical and industry experience, allowing clients to strengthen their key governance, risk management and compliance efforts while optimizing business performance. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing the planning, execution and reporting for risk management internal control and internal audit and identifying and executing on market and brand development opportunities for clients in the Insurance space of our Financial Services (FS) sector. This role will also support the growth of our insurance practice within GRCS by identifying client opportunities, contributing to m

title: Consultant - Pension Actuarial
description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.  KPMG’s Total Rewards and Actuarial Consulting (TRAC) team sits within KPMG’s Actuarial Practice, where we bring together actuaries and actuarial students in the areas of Pensions, Benefits, Life Insurance and Property & Casualty Insurance. The Actuarial Analyst will provide support on a variety of engagements and help drive the growth of the TRAC team. This role will involve participation in engagement teams providing pension, benefits, and other HR advice to a range of clients, related to:Financial statement auditsAccounting standards advisoryActuarial valuationsAcquisitions and divestituresRisk management  What you will doAs part of our TRAC team, the successful candidate will be responsible for the following:Analyze defined benefit plan financial statement disclosures as part of year-end audits under various accounting standardsCompile and analyze actuarial assumption data for audit clientsSupport projects such as acquisitions and valuations, as neededCalculate benefit entitlements for retiring and terminating pension plan members What you bring to the roleA successful candidate will possess a strong mixture of education, experience, skills and behaviors such

title: Senior Manager, Audit Quality Controls & Risk Management
description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.Your work matters. As part of our Audit & Assurance practice, you’ll deliver high‑quality work that supports trust, transparency, and confidence across both public and private organizations. We foster an environment of high challenge, where curiosity and critical thinking are encouraged.  Through high support, including progressive learning,  strong coaching, inclusive teamwork, and a shared commitment to integrity, we perform at our best – measuring what matters, recognizing impact, and continuously evolving to deliver excellence. This creates a culture where the best work happens when high challenge and high support work together.  That balance is how our values show up—in how we work, how we lead, and what we deliver.The team you are joining is an integral part of KPMG’s Department of Professional Practice (DPP). This team helps provide thought leadership and guidance on emerging standards and trends, leading and shaping key components of the firm’s System of Quality Management (SQM), including delivery of firmwide learning and representing the function in internal and external inspections. As part of our SQM team, you will collaborate with stakeholders across

title: Senior Translator, Linguistic Services
description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.   KPMG’s Linguistic Services team in Canada is looking to fill a Senior Translator position within its External Translation Team (client audit documents). This is a full-time permanent position based in Montreal and offered on a fully remote basis. To be considered for the Senior Translator role, you must have between five (5) and ten (10) years of experience in accounting or financial translation, including a minimum of five (5) years in a structured professional environment or within an accounting firm, and meet the other requirements listed below. Are you interested in working in a stimulating field as part of a high-performing team dedicated to quality and client satisfaction? Are you resourceful, motivated, and dynamic? We would love to hear from you! Send us your CV! What you will doTranslate various financial, accounting, and legal documents from English into French (and revise them when required), including financial statements, management reports, Management Reports of Fund Performance (MRFPs), and sustainability reports.Produce high-quality translations while meeting budget requirements and deadlines.Actively participate in the management and delivery o

title: Learning Coordinator - GLMS Tester
description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.The Training Learning Coordinator - GLMS Tester is responsible for supporting the end to end deployment of training programs by managing learning systems (GLMS), coordinating course delivery, and performing quality assurance testing. This role ensures that training is accurately built, thoroughly tested, and seamlessly delivered, providing a consistent and high-quality learner experience.Key ResponsibilitiesBuild and maintain course shells, sessions, and curricula in GLMSManage learner registrations, assignments, and completion trackingGenerate reports and monitor course data to ensure accuracy and completenessTroubleshoot system-related issues and liaise with learning tech teams when neededConduct end-to-end testing of courses prior to release (including navigation, functionality, and assessments)Validate that course operates correctly in the systemIdentify, document, and follow up on defects or inconsistenciesSupport sign-off processes to ensure courses meet quality standards before launchBuild and schedule virtual and in-person training sessionsSupport live training sessions, including troubleshooting technical issuesCoordinate logistics and ensure readiness of

title: Halifax Property Legal Assistant
description:
Do you thrive in a collaborative team setting and take pride in delivering exceptional client service? Are you energized by fast-paced, innovative work where every day offers opportunities to learn, contribute, and grow?McInnes Cooper is seeking a motivated and proactive Property Legal Assistant to join our team in our Halifax office. In this role, you will provide high-quality administrative and client support, helping to ensure our property clients receive exceptional service while contributing to a positive, engaged, and solutions-focused team environment.
Qualifications
The successful candidate will possess the following skill set:The ideal candidate will have a Legal Administration or Paralegal diploma; however, we recognize that historical and systemic barriers have shaped and limited access to traditional education, training, and employment pathways for many individuals, particularly those from marginalized communities. As such, we welcome applicants who bring a combination of skills, knowledge, and lived experience.Minimum one (1) year of experience in an administrative role; experience working in the area of real estate law would be considered an asset.Exceptional technology skills, with proficiency in all aspects of Microsoft Office and a genuine interest in keeping up to date with the newest technology.Excel in a demanding, fast-paced team environment, be highly organized & detail-oriented, and flexible to changing priorities.Exceptional communication skills, a spe

title: Director, Lifecycle and CRM, RBCx
description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

As Director, Lifecycle & CRM, you will lead the strategy and execution of how we communicate with clients across email, SMS, and owned digital channels. You will own lifecycle strategy, campaign development, and optimization—establishing the technology configuration and operational model that will scale with RBCx's growth. You will lead a team of 12 specialists, connecting their individual disciplines into a single, cohesive practice.

This role works in the office 3 to 4 days a week.

What will you do?

Lifecycle Strategy and CRM Technology

  • Own the strategy, build, and continuous optimization of client communication journeys across email and SMS—from onboarding and activation through engagement, retention, and win-back

  • Develop audience segmentation and targeting frameworks that ensure the right message reaches the right client at the right moment across multiple brands

  • B

title: Director, Media & Analytics, RBCx
description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

As Director, Media and Analytics, you will own our measurement infrastructure and paid media strategy across the RBCx practice. You'll oversee a unified team of analysts and media specialists, driving data-driven decisions from campaign strategy through real-time optimization. This role demands hands-on technical expertise combined with strategic capital deployment accountability.

This role works in the office 3 to 4 days a week.

What will you do?

Marketing Performance & Capital Deployment

  • Own the strategic framework for how our brands measure and optimize marketing effectiveness across all digital channels (paid search, social, programmatic, video, affiliate)

  • Develop measurement infrastructure strategy—tagging architecture, data pipelines, tracking solutions (GTM, server-side tagging), and platform health across our toolsets—that enables real-time, actionable insights

  • Establish da

title: Senior Manager, Customer Experience, Dr.Bill, RBCx
description:
Job Description

The RBCx Customer Experience & Fraud Management team expertly leverages diverse skill sets and customer-focused, innovative agile approaches to deliver
industry best outcomes in customer experience, and fraud management. The purpose of this role is to lead the Customer Experience team for Dr. Bill, providing coaching, guidance, and oversight to the team, with a specific focus on the customer experience. A part of Operations & Strategic Initiatives, the Customer Experience team supports the Portfolio Company in its strategic goals, focusing heavily on how to drive strong business results, providing actionable insights, and representing the voice of the customer.

Work location arrangements to be discussed at the time of the interview.

What will you do?

Lead the Dr. Bill Customer experience team

  • Manage a team of specialists responsible for FPOC customer contact (chat/email/phone) including customer escalations.

  • Provide managerial over

title: Technical Support Analyst
location: Montreal - 1000 Rue De La Gauchetière Ouest, Canada
description: Putting people first, every day BDO is a firm built on a foundation of positive relationships with our people and our clients. Each day, our professionals provide exceptional service, helping clients with advice and insight they can trust. In turn, we offer an award-winning environment that fosters a people-first culture with a high priority on your personal and professional growth. Your Opportunity Our Montreal office is looking for a Technical Support Analyst, to join the IT team and own the following responsibilities. Work as part of the End User Services team, providing a range of IT support services to internal customers within the Quebec region and across Canada. Set up desktops and laptops with Firm provided image, and configure the user profile which includes the installation of appropriate software, peripherals, etc. Provide support for hardware (laptops, printers, scanners), software (MS Office 365 and internal applications), etc. Deliver in-person, service desk queue, and remote desktop support to customers. Maintain supported computer inventory of hardware in the Asset Management Database. Utilize effective communication and listening skills with customers and colleagues to troubleshoot technology issues. Ability to prioritize tasks, and work in a fast-paced customer service environment. Understanding of networks and servers, previous exposure to a Windows network environment. Participate in IT projects when needed. Knowledge of iPhone, and Android devices. Positi

title: Co-op/Intern – Forensic Disputes & Investigation (FDI) Calgary or Vancouver | January – April 2027
description: Putting people first, every day BDO is a firm built on a foundation of positive relationships with our people and our clients. Each day, our professionals provide exceptional service, helping clients with advice and insight they can trust. In turn, we offer an award-winning environment that fosters a people-first culture with a high priority on your personal and professional growth. Your Opportunity Ready to sharpen your critical thinking, level up your analytical toolkit, and enhance your Excel-based financial modeling skills? Join BDO’s Forensic Disputes & Investigation (“FDI”) team in Calgary or Vancouver and apply your classroom learning to complex, real-world business and legal challenges. This is a net new role and as a Co-op Student or Intern, you will: Develop a forensic mindset, learning how to detect patterns, identify red flags, and connect financial, operational, and legal evidence. Strengthen your financial modeling skills in Excel, working on loss quantification, economic analyses, and scenario-based models. Gain hands-on exposure to fraud investigations, commercial insurance claims, and litigation support files. Grow your communication, client service, and teamwork skills by collaborating with experienced professionals and clients across Western Canada and nationally. This is a unique opportunity to explore the world of investigative and forensic accounting while being mentored by a high-performing national team of specialists. What you’ll be doing You will be
